channel.title: BinderBrief Daily
channel.description: Daily commercial insurance and underwriting automation insights.

item.title: Lemonade chief insurance officer sells $283k in shares By Investing.com
item.description:
PART 1: THE BINDERBRIEF SUMMARY Lemonade, Inc. Chief Insurance Officer John Sheldon Peters sold $283,257 in common stock over two transactions at prices ranging from $52.94 to $53.17 per share. The first sale was to cover tax withholding on vested Restricted Stock Units, and the second was made under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. Lemonade’s Q1 2026 results exceeded revenue and adjusted gross profit estimates, supporting its expansion into renters insurance markets and indicating improved underwriting performance and margin potential.
PART 2: CONTACT EXTRACTION Name: John Sheldon Peters, Company: Lemonade, Inc., Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Insurance Officer at Lemonade, Inc.

item.title: AM Best to Hold Analytical Briefing on How Insurers Are Using Artificial Intelligence
item.description:
PART 1: THE BINDERBRIEF SUMMARY AM Best announced a virtual analytical briefing on July 16, 2026, presenting findings from a survey of over 150 insurers and MGAs about their use of artificial intelligence in insurance. The briefing will cover AI applications in underwriting, claims, customer experience, risks, governance, and future expectations, featuring industry executives and AM Best analysts. This event highlights how AI adoption is expected to improve underwriting accuracy, operational efficiency, and risk assessment, potentially affecting broker margins and carrier capacity in commercial insurance.
PART 2: CONTACT EXTRACTION Name: Aman Gour, Company: FurtherAI, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of FurtherAI, an AI technology provider in insurance. Name: Josh Hershman, Company: State of Connecticut, Email: N/A, Type: Government, Info: Connecticut Insurance Commissioner. Name: Chetan Kandhari, Company: Nationwide, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Vice President and Chief AI and Digital Transformation Officer at Nationwide. Name: Kaitlin Piasecki, Company: AM Best, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Industry research analyst at AM Best. Name: Sridhar Manyem, Company: AM Best, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Director, Industry Research and Analytics, AM Best and moderator of the briefing panel. Name: Christopher Sharkey, Company: AM Best, Email: christopher.sharkey@ambest.com, Type: Individual, Info: Associate Director, Public Relations at AM Best. Name: Al Slavin, Company: AM Best, Email: al.slavin@ambest.com, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Public Relations Specialist at AM Best.

item.title: Messaging Security Gateway Market to Reach USD 11.52 Billion by 2031, Driven by Rising Cybersecurity Threats and Cloud Adoption | Valuates Reports
item.description:
PART 1: THE BINDERBRIEF SUMMARY The global Messaging Security Gateway (MSG) market was valued at USD 6.86 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 11.52 billion by 2031, growing at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.8%. The market growth is driven by cloud-based and on-premises gateway deployments that enforce message filtering, encryption, threat detection, and compliance policies across corporate communication channels. This expansion improves carrier underwriting efficiency by reducing risk exposure from phishing, impersonation, data leakage, and compliance failures, while increasing broker margins through enhanced demand for secure messaging solutions in regulated sectors.
PART 2: CONTACT EXTRACTION Name: N/A, Company: Valuates Reports, Email: sales@valuates.com, Type: Company, Info: Market research firm providing analysis on technology and cybersecurity industries.

item.title: Cyber covers every business risk. Personal fraud victims are on their own
item.description:
NAVACORD executive Patrick Bourk highlighted that current cyber insurance products focus on companies, not individuals, despite growing AI-driven personal fraud cases involving losses up to $100,000 and additional device remediation costs. These frauds employ AI technologies such as deepfake video calls and personalized scams, exploiting extended timelines and compromised devices for financial fraud. The personal cyber risk gap reduces insurance coverage for individuals, limiting consumer protection and potentially increasing uninsured losses in the market.
Name: Patrick Bourk, Company: Navacord, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Vice president of cyber and professional lines at Navacord

item.description:
PART 1: THE BINDERBRIEF SUMMARY Lemonade, Inc. Chief Insurance Officer John Sheldon Peters sold $283,257 in common stock over two transactions at prices ranging from $52.94 to $53.17 per share. The first sale was to cover tax withholding on vested Restricted Stock Units, and the second was made under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. PART 2: CONTACT EXTRACTION Name: John Sheldon Peters, Company: Lemonade, Inc., Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Insurance Officer at Lemonade, Inc.

PART 1: THE BINDERBRIEF SUMMARY The global Messaging Security Gateway (MSG) market was valued at USD 6.86 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 11.52 billion by 2031, growing at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.8%. The market growth is driven by cloud-based and on-premises gateway deployments that enforce message filtering, encryption, threat detection, and compliance policies across corporate communication channels. This expansion improves carrier underwriting efficiency by reducing risk exposure from phishing, impersonation, data leakage, and compliance failures, while increasing broker margins through enhanced demand for secure messaging solutions in regulated sectors.

item.title: Cyber covers every business risk. Personal fraud victims are on their own
item.description:
NAVACORD executive Patrick Bourk highlighted that current cyber insurance products focus on companies, not individuals, despite growing AI-driven personal fraud cases involving losses up to $100,000 and additional device remediation costs. These frauds employ AI technologies such as deepfake video calls and personalized scams, exploiting extended timelines and compromised devices for financial fraud. The personal cyber risk gap reduces insurance coverage for individuals, limiting consumer protection and potentially increasing uninsured losses in the market.
Name: Patrick Bourk, Company: Navacord, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Vice president of cyber and professional lines at Navacord
